description_long: This helmet is a cylindrical, flat-topped medieval-style great helm, primarily constructed from dark metal with a matte finish. The front features a prominent brass or bronze cross-shaped reinforcement, with a horizontal slit for vision and a series of small round ventilation holes below. A white cross motif is visible on the lower left side of the front, adding a decorative and symbolic element. The sides and back are solid, with visible rivets and panel seams, and lack any significant openings or adornments. The overall design is robust and utilitarian, evoking historical European knight armor. The interior is likely hollow to accommodate a human head, with no visible padding or lining from the exterior.
description: A dark, cylindrical medieval great helm with a brass cross on the front, vision slit, and a white cross motif. The helmet is solid and robust, with small ventilation holes and visible rivets.
description_view_0: Front view shows the brass cross, vision slit, white cross motif, and ventilation holes.
description_view_1: Left-rear angle reveals the solid side and back panels with rivets and the edge of the brass cross.
description_view_2: Rear view displays a plain, solid back with rivets and no openings or decorations.
description_view_3: Right-rear angle highlights the side panel, rivets, and partial view of the brass cross and vision slit.
attribute_ventilation_pattern: Multiple small round holes arranged in two vertical rows on the lower front left and right sides.
attribute_graphic_design: A white cross motif on the lower left front; otherwise, the helmet is plain with a solid color and brass cross reinforcement.
